title: Intricately Simmered Greek Lettuce Nimono
description: A floral and nostalgic Greek dish featuring lettuce simmered in a flavorful broth, served in a bento box. This no-onion-no-garlic-friendly recipe is perfect for a light lunch.
instructions: Rinse the lettuce leaves and separate them into individual leaves.
In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the lemon juice, chicken broth, thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper to the saucepan.
Bring the broth to a simmer and let cook for 5 minutes.
Add the lettuce leaves to the saucepan and simmer for 10 minutes, or until they are tender.
While the lettuce is simmering, prepare the bento boxes by placing a small amount of cooked rice in the bottom of each box.
Once the lettuce is cooked, remove it from the saucepan with a slotted spoon and place it on top of the rice in the bento boxes.
Crumble the feta cheese over the top of the lettuce and serve immediately.
